summary: Frontend-only testing of the refactored Sidebar.js (Workspace Switcher pattern). Verified admin, platform_owner and staff flows on both desktop and mobile. All core behaviors from the spec are working as designed.
verified_behaviors: Admin (SKAdmin): workspace-switcher visible at top of sidebar showing current workspace icon + label + subtitle.
Admin: clicking switcher opens data-testid='workspace-dropdown' listing workspace-option-pos, workspace-option-kds, workspace-option-workforce.
Admin: selecting Workforce shows ONLY Shift Scheduler / Attendance / Timesheets in middle nav. Dashboard still pinned at top, Settings pinned above user/logout, logout at very bottom.
Admin: selecting Point of Sale restores all 8 POS items (POS Terminal, Orders, Menu, Tables, Cash Drawer, Printers, Reports, Audit Log).
Direct URL /workforce/shifts auto-switches the workspace: switcher reads 'Workforce · Schedule · HR · Payroll' and localStorage.heva_active_workspace = 'workforce'.
Workspace persists across reloads via localStorage key heva_active_workspace (confirmed after hard reload).
Mobile drawer (<768px) renders the same switcher + Dashboard pinned + Settings pinned above user + Logout pinned at bottom (screenshot confirms).

Platform owner (platform_owner): sidebar shows the old platform menu unchanged — Dashboard, Businesses, Platform Reports, Categories, Settings; NO workspace switcher and NO workspace-static. Logo subtitle reads 'PLATFORM'. Logout works and redirects to /login.
Logout from admin sidebar successfully redirects to /login.
Settings link appears only for admin role, pinned just above the user/logout block (not present in the 'nav' section for platform owner, rendered via platform menu instead).
action_items: Clarification only (not a regression): Staff user 'user' is routed to /heva-ops/shifts which uses a separate layout component — Sidebar.js is NOT rendered for staff in the current app. Therefore the spec item 'Staff login — sidebar shows their enabled workspaces only ... workspace-static for single workspace' cannot be observed in the live app. The STAFF_WORKSPACES definition and workspace-static code path in Sidebar.js is effectively dead code unless staff routing is changed to use Sidebar. If this is intentional (staff uses Heva Ops bottom-nav layout), consider removing STAFF_WORKSPACES to reduce dead code; if not, update staff routing to render Sidebar.js for roles == 'staff' with workforce-only access.
critical_code_review_comments: Sidebar.js (~555 lines): still within size budget but close to the 700-line guideline. Consider splitting WorkspaceSwitcher, SidebarShell, and workspace definitions into separate files for future maintainability.

Workspace definitions (ADMIN_WORKSPACES / STAFF_WORKSPACES) duplicate the same icons and path metadata across admin/staff — can be consolidated into one source of truth with role-based filtering to remove ~30 lines of repetition (DRY).

The query_selector('text=Menu') false-positives during testing were caused by dashboard-tile text (e.g., 'Cash', 'Orders') — not a real bug, but confirms nav items should be queried via data-testid (already implemented as 'nav-<label>') rather than text. Good.
STAFF_WORKSPACES.workforce.requires uses ['workforce'] which relies on hasFeature('workforce'). Ensure feature flag naming stays aligned with backend keys.

success_rate.frontend: 100% of testable spec items on admin + platform_owner + mobile drawer

test_credentials: SKAdmin/saswata@123 (admin, all modules), platform_owner/admin123 (platform), user/user123 (staff — routed to /heva-ops, does NOT render Sidebar.js)
context_for_next_testing_agent: The new Sidebar workspace switcher is working correctly for admin and platform_owner on both desktop and mobile. Staff role bypasses Sidebar.js entirely (uses Heva Ops layout) — so the workspace-static code path in Sidebar.js is unreachable with the current user/user123 account. If future changes require staff to use Sidebar, seed a staff user routed to /dashboard and retest the workspace-static rendering.
